observations.recommendation:
1.16 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Require, the GSO and the Inventory Committee to account all
unserviceable property, and thereafter be disposed by the Disposal
Committee, in accordance with Chapter 6 of the Handbook on
Property and Supply Management System;
b) Require the Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of
complete physical count on all types of property, plant and
equipment, and prepare the prescribed annual RPCPPE thereon
which shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for
reconciliation of records, and adjust the books accordingly; and
c) Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO
to update the affected records i.e. PCs and PPELCs.

2.8 In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we strongly recommended and the
Management agreed to instruct the following:
a) Immediate submission of all unsubmitted reports to the Office of
Auditor such as the RCI duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer,
ROD, DVs, payrolls, together with their supporting documents;
b) Initiate the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for each
transfer, whether or not maintained in separate bank accounts;
c) Make the necessary adjustments and establish of the necessary
accounts, to correct the ledgers, trial balance, and the financial
statements; and
d) Finally, submit to the Auditor’s Office a schedule of all the
transfers/subisidies actually received by the Management including
their bank accounts, if any.

3.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate the preparation and prompt submission of the
monthly BRS to the Audit Team for their review pursuant to Section
74 PD No. 1445 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011. In case of
delayed production of the bank statements, securing a snap-shots as of
a given date will do.

4.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit separate RODs for
each cash advances, together with all the necessary supporting
liquidation papers, to the Municipal Accountant immediately
after the purpose has been served; and
b) Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly
mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs, together with
the necessary attachments, within the time frame prescribed by
the Commission.

5.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal
to establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex-
Disaggregated data and gender statistics and consider such in the
preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget pursuant to PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to gather and address all
pressing gender issues within the Municipality.
1.Create the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct the inventory on the physical count of its road network and prepare a report thereon;
2.Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local roads network accounts based on the result of the physical count; and
3.Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

4.We recommended that the Management create a separate special account in the General Fund (SAGF) for the BGCM Fund through an ordinance by the local sangguniang pursuant to Section 313 of RA No. 7160 for purposes of easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the allocation thereof.
5.A monthly report on BGCM fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125; and

6.A quarterly Fund Receipt and Utilization Report (FRUR) for BGCM Fund as special account for General Fund and a year-end FRUR, which contains the summary of transactions for the year as integral part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance for purposes of proper recording in the books of accounts
7.We recommended that the Municipal Accountant attach the corresponding JEV, numbered and properly filled-out, on every disbursement voucher and payroll paid.

8.We therefore reiterated our previous recommendation that the Management strictly submit to the Office of the Auditor copies of the contract/purchase order including all the pertinent supporting documents thereto as mandated in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection and/or approval.

9.We therefore reiterated our previous recommendation that the Management require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, thereafter submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely audit verification and review thereof.

10. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit separate RODs for each of the various cash advances together with all the necessary supporting liquidation instruments to the Municipal Accountant immediately after the purpose has been served; and

11. Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs together with the necessary attachments within the time frame prescribed by the Commission.

12. We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to the provisions COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

13. We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders (POs)/ Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the procurement of goods within five (5) working days from the execution thereof and within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006, which precluded the Audit.

6. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, and submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely verification and audit.

7. We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders (POs)/ Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the procurement of goods within five (5) working days from the execution thereof and within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006, which precluded the Audit.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a) Create the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct the inventory on the physical count of its road network and prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local roads network accounts based on the result of the physical count; and
c) Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that Management create a separate special account in the General Fund (SAGF) for the BGCM Fund through an ordinance by the local Sangguniang pursuant to Section 313 of RA No. 7160 for purposes of easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the allocation thereof.

We recommended that Management prepare:
a) A monthly report on BGCM fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125; and
b) A quarterly Fund Receipt and Utilization Report (FRUR) for BGCM Fund as special account for General Fund and a year-end FRUR, which contains the summary of transactions for the year as integral part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance for purposes of proper recording in the books of accounts
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant attach the corresponding JEV, numbered and properly filled-out, on every disbursement voucher and payroll paid.

We therefore reiterated our previous recommendation that Management strictly submit to the Office of the Auditor copies of the contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents thereto as mandated in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection and/or approval.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, thereafter submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely audit verification and review thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit a separate ROD for each of the various cash advances together with all the necessary supporting liquidating instruments to the Municipal Accountant immediately after the purpose has been served; and
b) Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs together with the necessary attachments within the time frame prescribed by the Commission.

5. We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to the provisions COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee who will facilitate the conduct of a complete physical inventory initially and subsequently every year thereafter.
b. Prepare promptly and completely the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) thereon.
c. Instruct the Supply Officer /Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

1.6 We reiterated to recommend that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee of at least three (3) or more
responsible officials to include always the Property Officer and a
representative from Accounting Office;
b. Instruct and supervise the conduct of physical inventory count of
all properties of the Municipality with a witness of a
representative from the Office of the Auditor;
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property ledger cards
and reconcile with the report of the Municipal Accountant; and
d. Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly signed by the committee
members to the Office of the Auditor.

2.6 We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to
prepare the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2019 in pursuance to
the provisions of COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 and
submit thereafter to the Office of Auditor for auditorial review and
evaluation.

3.4 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and
Development officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the
beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the
yearend, thereafter submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of
COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely audit verification and review
thereof.

4.5 We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the
Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders(POs)/Contracts and
Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the procurement
of goods within five (5) working days from the execution thereof and
within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively,
pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006, which precluded
the Audit

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, and submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely verification and audit.

We recommended that the Accounting Office and Treasury Office continue to be compliant in the deduction of GSIS, Philhealth and Pag-ibig Fund premium contributions and Loan Amortizations and the remittance thereof including the Government share within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare all identified unsubmitted RCDs and submit to the Auditor immediately together with their supporting documents in compliance to Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer or the MPDC, to submit the complete list of all PPAs in CY 2018;
b. Provide the Audit Team with updated information as to the status of the publicity of PPAs; and
c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the required annual LDRRMFIP for CY 2018 be prepared in adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 or dated September 12, 2012, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Auditor’s Office the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF, as well as the periodic reports required Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended that Management a. Form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; and b. Comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment.

1.6 We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to the provisions COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

2.4 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, and submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely verification and audit.

3.6 We recommended that the Accounting Office and Treasury Office continue to be compliant in the deduction of GSIS, Philhealth and Pag-ibig Fund premium contributions and Loan Amortizations and the remittance thereof including the Government share within the prescribed period.

4.3 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare all identified unsubmitted RCDs and submit to the Auditor immediately together with their supporting documents in compliance to Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

5.6 We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer or the MPDC, to submit the complete list of all PPAs in CY 2018;
b. Provide the Audit Team with updated information as to the status of the publicity of PPAs; and
c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

6.6 We recommended that the required annual LDRRMFIP for CY 2018 be prepared in adherence to the provisions COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

6.11 We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Auditor’s Office the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF, as well as the periodic reports required Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002.

7.8 We recommended that Management
a.) form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; and
b.) comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment.

8.8 We recommended that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee who will facilitate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of all PPE initially and subsequently every year thereafter.
b. Prepare promptly and completely the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) thereon.
b. Instruct the Supply Officer / Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

9.7 We recommended that responsible persons should settle their audit suspensions in the sum of ₱915,896.01 as soon as possible. Further, Management should revisit existing disbursement controls of the LGU and install appropriate measures designed to safeguard public funds from possible misuse.

We recommended that Management initiate the effort to create Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all PPE properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 and 114of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. It is further recommended that the LCE directs Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), respectively in compliance to Section 114 of same Manual.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the issuance of official receipt to each payor as proof of acknowledgement of receipt of payment and the preparation of report of collection thereof that would show the details of payors in compliance with the Section 68 paragraph 1 of PD 1445.

We recommend that Management strictly implement the mandatory deductions pertaining to GSIS, Pag-ibig, Philhealth and withholding tax for personnel and remitting the same on time in compliance with the above-cited provisions of laws and regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer be directed to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.

We recommend that that the management should furnish the Audit Team a copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection/approval/execution.

We recommended that management should complete the physical inventory of is properties, plant and equipment and submit the related Report of the Physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) using the prescribed format to the Auditor for verification in compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

We recommended that Management initiate the effort to create Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 and 114of NGAS Manual for LGUs, determine the existence and accuracy of PPE.
It is further recommended that the LCE directs Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), respectively in compliance to Section 114 of same Manual.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the issuance of official receipt to each payor as proof of acknowledgement of receipt of payment and the preparation of report of collection thereof that would show the details of payors in compliance with the Section 68 paragraph 1 of PD 1445.

We recommend that Management strictly implement the mandatory deductions pertaining to GSIS, Pag-ibig, Philhealth and  withholding tax for personnel and remitting the same on time  in compliance with the above-cited provisions of laws and regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer be directed to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.

Recommendation:
We recommend that that the management should furnish the Audit Team a copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection/approval/execution.

It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:
demonstrating leadership by example;
training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and
Enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.

It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/implementing the project should faithfully perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

We recommend that the management should furnish COA copy of any contract/ purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12,2009 within (5) days  after perfection/ approval/ execution.

It is recommended that the management should continue to adhere to the existing rules and regulations regarding proper deductions and complete remittances of amount due to GSIS, HDMF, PhilHealth and BIR.

We recommend that Management should review the deductions and the remittances made for GSIS premiums/contributions in the previous years. Update the records of its members in the GSIS office. Make a representation to the GSIS Iligan Branch Office, Iligan City and conduct a reconciliation of their records to establish the correct obligations for GSIS premiums and contributions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to initiate the conduct of physical inventory of PPE within the target period..
observations.reason: Still in the process of updating records.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the issuance of official receipt to each payor as proof of acknowledgement of receipt of payment and the preparation of report of collection thereof that would show the details of payors in compliance with the Section 68 paragraph 1 of PD 1445 and Sections 16, 22 and 23 of  NGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The mgt tried their best to educate concerned taxpayer.
observations.reason: Failure to collect the actual  Real property tax required for the individual  taxpayer.

observations.recommendation:
1.9	We recommended that management should complete the physical inventory of is properties, plant and equipment and submit the related Report of the Physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) using the prescribed format to the Auditor for verification in compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
1.10	Further, we recommended that the Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to update their property records/ledger cards and reconcile existing records.

It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:
demonstrating leadership by example;
training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and
Enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.

It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/implementing the project should faithfully perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the management should furnish COA copy of any contract/ purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12,2009 within (5) days  after perfection/ approval/ execution.

It is recommended that the management should continue to adhere to the existing rules and regulations regarding proper deductions and complete remittances of amount due to GSIS, HDMF, PhilHealth and BIR.

It is recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees who are determined as persons responsible/liable should immediately settle/comply with the requirements of the subject of NS/ND/NC.

We  recommend that management should initiate the effort to conduct a yearly inventory of their properties to be able submit the related Report of the Physical Count on Property, Plant and Equipment  (RPCPPE) in compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. Likewise, coordination from all concerned officials shall be solicited in order to properly determine the existence and true value of properties under their accountability which shall eventually result to a more fair presentation of the  agency's fixed assets.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer are directed to update their records and effect the necessary adjustments in the books within the target date.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer are still in the process of reconciling their records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to prepare the required Bank Reconciliation Statements to come up with a reliable balance of Cash in Bank Account.
observations.managementAction: Copies of PO’s, contracts are submitted to the Audit Team..

observations.recommendation: Cause for the conduct orientation among concerned personnel on GAD in particular to the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM JC 2012-01.Specifically, make sure that proper budgeting, accounting and reporting of GAD Fund are duly complied with and, that, the fund can only be utilized according  to the intended purpose(s).
observations.managementAction: The management tried their best to identify the proper charging of expenditures.
observations.reason: Some GAD projects were properly accounted for.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated                        May 18, 1995.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC have already initiated internet access
observations.reason: Unexpended/Unobligated balance of the QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE are transferred to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” (Code 438) in the Trust Fund books.
